text.title: Per-feature delta update protocol
text.summary: project-spec/ updates land in the same PR that ships the feature, not in a follow-up. The readiness.md checkbox enforces it.

rule.statement: Any project-spec/ change required by a feature MUST be committed in the same PR that ships the feature code.
rule.rationale: Follow-up PRs vanish under "I will do it later" and the spec drifts. Same-PR ties code and contract atomically.

example.note: Feature F0042 adds an email-verification flow. The PR contains code changes + new lines under project-spec/auth.md ("email verification required for tier ≥ solo") + new entry in project-spec/business-rules.md ("BR-019: unverified accounts cannot upgrade tier").
Feature F0042 ships email verification; project-spec/auth.md still describes the pre-feature state. Reviewer approves because "the spec PR is coming next week".

rule.statement: If a feature genuinely has no spec impact (pure perf tweak, copy fix, internal refactor with unchanged contract), readiness.md MUST state "no spec impact" with a one-line reason.
rule.rationale: The escape hatch is intentional but loud. Forcing a reason flushes out features that the author thinks have no impact but actually do.

rule.statement: Reviewer MUST check the spec diff against the code diff; mismatches block merge.
rule.rationale: The author has a blind spot — they wrote the code and the spec from the same mental model. A second pair of eyes catches the gap.
